Name: Eurybates, Herald of Odysseus
Anecdote: 'Of all his heralds, Odysseus valued Eurybates most — round-shouldered, dark, and sharper than he looked.'

Name: Calchas, Prophet of Apollo
Effect: On enter: Look at the top 2 cards of your deck. Put one into your hand and the other at the bottom.
Anecdote: 'He alone could read the flight of birds, and told the Greeks what the gods demanded before the fleet could sail.'

Name: Sinon the Deceiver
Effect: On enter: Move this card to the opponent's side of this location. While on top: The opponent must play with their hand revealed.
Anecdote: 'He stood before the Trojans, hands bound, and told them exactly enough of the truth to be believed.'

Name: Greek Soldiers
Effect: When Greek Soldiers move to the opponent's side of this location: For that opponent, you may destroy up to five of their beings with Power [1] or less.
Anecdote: 'In the darkness of the horse's belly we waited, crouched, armed, silent. Sinon's torch was the signal.'

Name: Dolon the Scout
Effect: On enter: Move this card to the opponent's side of this location. While on top: Once per turn you may look at the top card of the opponent's deck and choose to put it on the bottom.
Anecdote: 'He wore the wolf-skin and went out alone into the dark, certain no one would catch him. He was wrong.'

Name: Menelaus, the Wronged King
Effect: While on top: This card has +2 Power for each card the opponent controls more than you at this location.
Anecdote: 'Ten years. Not for glory. Not for Agamemnon's pride. He simply refused to be the man whose wife left and who did nothing.'

Name: Camp Guard at the Ships
Effect: On enter: Draw 1 card. If you control a card on the opponent's side of the location, draw 2 cards instead.
Anecdote: 'His orders were simple: watch the plain, and shout if the fires moved.'

Name: Epeius, Builder of the Horse
Anecdote: 'Athena herself guided his hands. He wept as he built it, knowing what it was for.'

Name: Odysseus
Effect: While on top: Once per turn, you may move Odysseus to another location.
Anecdote: 'He is never at a loss. That is what makes him dangerous.'

Name: Patroclus
Effect: On enter and if Achilles is in play: For each opponent destroy an enemy Being here with Power equal to or less than Patroclus.
Anecdote: 'He put on Achilles' armour, and the Trojans broke before him — until they didn't.'

Name: The Trojan Horse
Effect: On enter: Move this card to the opponent's side of this location. Whenever it moves to the opponent's side: You may move any number of your Humans with it. Flip them face-down; they have −6 Power.
Anecdote: 'They brought it inside themselves. That was always the plan.'

Name: Ajax, the Great
Effect: While on top: Your Beings here cannot be moved by opponent card effects.
Anecdote: 'He gave ground for nothing and no one. When the others fled to the ships, Ajax walked backwards, facing the enemy.'

Name: Agamemnon, King of Mycenae
Effect: While on top: The opponent can have a maximum of 3 cards on their side of this location.
Anecdote: 'He was not the greatest warrior, nor the wisest. He was simply the one no one could ignore — and he never let them forget it.'

Name: Achilles
Effect: On enter: For each opponent, destroy an enemy Being here with Power equal to or less than Achilles. If Patroclus is in the underworld: For each opponent, destroy the strongest enemy Being here instead.
Anecdote: 'He knew he would not return. He chose to come anyway.'

Name: Diomedes, the God-Smiter
Effect: While on top: For each opponent, their strongest deity's power here is counted as 0.
Anecdote: 'He drove his spear into Ares, and the war god screamed. Even gods can bleed.'
